messages:
{"parts":[{"content":"Challenges at the recipient level\
\
•Most donor hospitals are in the private sector with homegrown  abdominal organ Tx programs

•Anticipatedly – they prioritize abdominal organs over thoracic

•Over hydration and unrestricted fluid administration to drop sodium levels for brain death declaration leads to congested lungs and distended hearts .

•The wanton maladministration of vasoconstrictors and inotropes further harm these organs .

•Any organ not accepted locally is distributed within the state and then nationally by a central body NOTTO

•Abdominal organ Tx teams prefer to operate at night to optimize theater usage by day

•Thus national  alerts have a short response time ; this leads to organ wastage, with alerts terminated rather than distributed.

•Opportunity exists in creating a system that maximise utilisation.

•","timestamp":"2026-02-09T00:11:16.998525Z","part_kind":"user-prompt"}],"timestamp":null,"instructions":"","kind":"request","run_id":null,"metadata":null}
